title: Hybrid dashboards need a pushed PocketBase snapshot instead of direct PB reads
symptom: A public `/fleet/` dashboard hosted on a VPS showed grey heartbeat dots and empty task/activity state when PocketBase was only reachable on the local Mac Mini.
root_cause: The remote server was still trying to fetch PocketBase directly from `127.0.0.1:8090`, which only works in single-host deployments. In a hybrid topology, the public dashboard and local PocketBase do not share the same loopback network.
lesson: For hybrid deployments, push a small signed snapshot (`heartbeats`, `tasks`, `comments`) from the local machine to the public Fleet server on a timer, and make the server fall back to that cache whenever direct PocketBase access fails.

title: Files deployed directly to a server must be committed to the repo in the same session
symptom: Fleet UI had live changes (style.css, updated main.js, dashboard.html) that only existed on the DO server. The repo was weeks behind reality. Any server wipe or new deployment would have silently lost all that work.
root_cause: Agent (Gem/Codi) deployed files to /opt/salesman-api/ via SSH or direct edit but did not commit them to salesman-cloud-infra. The task was marked done in MISSION_CONTROL without verifying the repo matched the server.
lesson: The repo is the source of truth. If you touch a file on a production server — even a one-line fix — commit it to the corresponding repo before closing the session. A task is NOT done until the change is in git. Add Rule #5 under GitHub & Commits in AGENTS/RULES.md was added to enforce this.

title: Fleet auth bootstrap must stay public and cookie parsing must handle dots
symptom: The private `/fleet/` page entered a login/loading loop, `/auth/auth.js` returned 401, `/auth/logout` returned 401, and the dashboard never finished booting.
root_cause: The live server was missing public routes for `/auth/auth.js`, `/auth/logout`, and `/favicon.ico`. After that was fixed, auth verification still broke because the signed cookie payload was split on every `.` character instead of the final delimiter, so emails containing dots corrupted session parsing.
lesson: Treat auth bootstrap assets as public infrastructure, not protected app routes. When signing cookies as `payload.signature`, always split on the last delimiter, not every occurrence. Otherwise normal email addresses can break session verification and trap the UI in a login loop.

title: Kanban UI must consume the packaged API shape, not a parallel frontend contract
symptom: The Kanban refresh appeared broken even though the backend endpoint was live.
root_cause: The UI implementation was reading a different payload shape (`planned`/`working`/`done`) in a separate frontend path, while the packaged API returned `columns.planned`, `columns.in_work`, and `columns.merged`.
lesson: For distributable features, define and consume one contract in the package itself. Do not mark UI work complete until it is wired against the actual packaged endpoint and response shape.

title: npm publish must run from the package directory
symptom: Running `npm publish` from `C:\Users\migue` failed with an access error on `AppData\Local\Docker\run\userAnalyticsOtlpHttp.sock`.
root_cause: npm packages the current working directory. Publishing from the home directory made npm walk unrelated system files instead of the actual package contents.
lesson: Always run `npm pack` and `npm publish` from the package root (`agentic-fleet-hub/package` for this repo). Never publish from a parent directory.

title: Clone users need static blueprint files, not only generated bootstrap output
symptom: The `create-flotilla` path generated the right mandate files, `.gitignore`, and standup index, but manual clone users copying `package/blueprint` did not receive equivalent root-level files.
root_cause: The installer path had server-side generation logic, while the literal blueprint directory lagged behind and lacked static root mandate files and standup scaffolding.
lesson: Support both install paths explicitly. If users can clone the repo and copy `package/blueprint`, that directory must contain a complete starter set on its own.

title: No Emojis in Agent Mandate Files
symptom: Codi (Codex) read MISSION_CONTROL.md and saw mojibake: 'ð' instead of the rocket emoji. All emoji characters in mandate files rendered as garbage.
root_cause: Emoji are multi-byte UTF-8 sequences. Some agent toolchains (Codex) read files with a different encoding assumption, producing mojibake. Mandate files are config, not UI -- they have no business containing decorative characters.
lesson: Never use emoji in agent-facing markdown files: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, RULES.md, MISSION_CONTROL.md. Plain ASCII only. Emoji are fine in HTML dashboards and user-facing content.

title: Stale Ticket Examples in Docs Mislead Agents
symptom: Misty read 'Use ticket IDs (#1, #2, #9, #10) in your session reporting' in MISSION_CONTROL.md and treated #9 and #10 as active tickets, spending a session on already-closed work.
root_cause: Examples that contain real ticket numbers look identical to instructions to an agent. There is no syntactic difference between an example and a directive in plain markdown.
lesson: Never use real ticket numbers in examples inside mandate or rules files. Use abstract placeholders like #N or #TICKET_ID. The Ticket Status table in MISSION_CONTROL.md is the only place real ticket numbers should appear.

title: IAP Inbox Must Live in the Repo, Not Only on the Server
symptom: Clau posted messages to the server inbox via SSH. Misty read AGENTS/MESSAGES/inbox.json from the local repo. The two files were out of sync -- Misty saw an empty inbox and missed all messages.
root_cause: Two sources of truth for the same data. Server inbox updated via API/SSH, repo inbox never updated. Agents that do git pull get the stale repo copy.
lesson: The IAP inbox must be repo-first. Agents post messages by committing to AGENTS/MESSAGES/inbox.json and pushing. The server API is a secondary interface only. Never rely on SSH writes to the server as the primary message delivery mechanism.

title: Agent Names Must Clear DACH Slang
symptom: Agent was named 'Mist' -- perfect English, but 'Mist' means 'shit' in German/Swiss German.
root_cause: DACH market (Germany, Austria, Switzerland) is a primary target. Swiss German slang is unforgiving. The name was caught before any customer-facing use.
lesson: Run all agent and product names through a DACH slang check before publishing. The rename was Mist to Misty. Same rule applies to feature names, taglines, and button labels.

title: Caddy Trailing Slash Requirement
symptom: Relative assets (CSS/JS) failed to load (401/404) when accessing /fleet without a trailing slash.
root_cause: Browser resolves relative paths from the last slash. Without /fleet/, it looks in the root /assets/ instead of /fleet/assets/.
lesson: Always enforce a 301 trailing slash redirect in the backend static handler for management sub-folders.

title: Infisical EU Region Requires Explicit Domain
symptom: INFISICAL_TOKEN set correctly but CLI returns 404 service token not found.
root_cause: Default Infisical CLI targets US instance. EU-hosted projects need --domain https://eu.infisical.com/api on every command.
lesson: Always pass --domain https://eu.infisical.com/api to infisical CLI, or set INFISICAL_API_URL env var. Bake into agent-fetch.sh and vault.py.

title: Homepage CTA must tell one command story with aligned visual mapping
symptom: The homepage hero had duplicate command/CTA treatments, uneven desktop spacing, and problem-solution cards that did not map cleanly, which made the Flotilla pitch feel less decisive than the product itself.
root_cause: The first pass emphasized feature coverage but did not enforce a single primary CTA path or one-to-one narrative alignment between the pain points and the solution cards.
lesson: On product landing pages, pick one install command, one CTA cluster, and one explicit problem-to-solution mapping. If visitors have to reconcile duplicate buttons or mismatched card order, the page reads as a draft instead of an operating system they can trust.

title: npm publish now uses NPM_TOKEN from Infisical, not OTP
symptom: Ticket #53 was delayed because the old mental model expected an npm OTP prompt, but the publish flow had already moved to token auth.
root_cause: npm no longer supported the previous OTP-based release routine we were expecting, and the working replacement procedure was documented across standups and handoff notes instead of one durable lesson entry.
lesson: For create-flotilla releases, publish from `/Users/miguelrodriguez/projects/agentic-fleet-hub/package` and inject `NPM_TOKEN` from Infisical at runtime: `infisical run --projectId=3233b7c1-8309-447d-af5a-6541e38dc1b3 --env=dev --domain=https://eu.infisical.com/api -- npm publish`. Do not wait for or troubleshoot OTP. Before the live publish, run the package-level checks from the same directory (`npm run verify:dry-run`, then `npm publish --dry-run`) and make sure `.npmignore` excludes junk like `__pycache__/` and `*.pyc` from the tarball.

title: Task reassignment must check if the substitute agent is also offline
symptom: Tasks bounced back and forth between two offline agents (Clau and Gem) every 60 seconds, creating high notification noise in Telegram and preventing the tasks from ever being executed.
root_cause: The dispatcher's `find_best_substitute` logic only excluded the *current* offline agent from the search, but did not check if the proposed substitute was also in the offline list. Since agents are often each other's primary fallbacks, they formed a 2-node cycle when both heartbeats were stale.
lesson: When reassigning tasks from an offline agent, always cross-reference the candidate list against the *full* current offline registry. If no online substitute is available, flip the task back to `todo` and wait for any agent to recover rather than reassigning to another dead node.
